name: Joachim Schiller
cv: Joachim Schiller, born in 2001, is a German author who has been writing since his early teens. He published his debut novel, 'Der Schatz der Welten: Untergang', in 2021, which became the first installment of a highly acclaimed fantasy trilogy. The series is known for its intricate world-building and complex characters. Schiller's work has been praised for its blend of adventure and philosophical depth, making him a rising star in contemporary German literature.

name: Paul Steinbeck
cv: Paul Steinbeck is the pen name of the author who was born in 1967 in Ravensburg, Upper Swabia. His real name is Hubert Romer. Paul Steinbeck is a native of Upper Swabia and lives in the Stuttgart region. He studied History, Geography, and Media Communication at the University of Trier, earning a Magister Artium degree.

name: Eléna Vásárhelyi
cv: BA Comparative Literature, ELTE Budapest (2004–2008). Literary translator, Hungarian–German–English (2008–2018). Debut novel A Határ Innenső Oldala (This Side of the Border), 2018. Published by NeubrandenBook since 2020. Three-time Hungarian Book of the Year shortlistee. Translated into 11 languages.

name: Finn Broekhuis
cv: BSc Physics, University of Amsterdam (2009–2013). Science journalist, NRC Handelsblad and De Ingenieur (2013–2021). Debut novel De Codekloof (NeubrandenBook, 2022). Television option acquired by VPRO, 2023. Non-fiction contributor, WorldSkills 2022 special report, De Ingenieur.

name: Sarah McLintock
cv: MA History, University of St Andrews (2001–2005). Freelance journalist and essayist (2005–2013). The Salt Road (NeubrandenBook, 2014). The Tide Warden (NeubrandenBook, 2017) – Booker Prize longlist. What the Heron Knows (NeubrandenBook, 2021). The Ferryman's Daughter (NeubrandenBook, 2024). Teaches creative writing, University of Edinburgh.

name: Tobias Wengler
cv: MA History and Archival Studies, Universität Leipzig (1995–2001). Archivist, Stadtarchiv Leipzig (2001–2016). Das Schweigen der Akten (NeubrandenBook, 2017). Meridiane des Vergessens (NeubrandenBook, 2020). Die letzte Zeugin (NeubrandenBook, 2023). Literaturpreis Leipzig, 2018.

name: Amara Diallo
cv: Qualified electrician, City & Guilds (2010–2012). BA Creative Writing, Goldsmiths, University of London (2015–2018). Skills of Gold (NeubrandenBook, 2023) – international bestseller. The Voltage Between Us (NeubrandenBook, 2025). WorldSkills Ambassador, 2024.
